name: The Segfault Catacombs

enemies.name: Buffer Overflow
enemies.description: Writes past the end of the array. Writes past the end of reality.

enemies.name: Dangling Pointer
enemies.description: Points to memory that was freed. Points to nothing. Points to pain.

enemies.name: Use-After-Free
enemies.description: The memory was freed. But it remembers. And it's angry.

enemies.name: Undefined Behavior
enemies.description: The compiler said it was fine. The compiler lied.

enemies.name: Header Guard Failure
enemies.description: Included 47 times. Redefines everything. Compile time: ∞.

enemies.name: The Leak
enemies.description: malloc without free. Growing forever. Consuming all.

bosses.name: The Compiler
bosses.description: 57 errors. Fix one, get 200 more. It feeds on your corrections.

bosses.name: Kernel Panic
bosses.description: Not syncing. Not recovering. Not sorry.

traps.name: Double Free
traps.description: free(): double free detected in tcache 2

empty: Raw memory addresses are etched into the stone walls.
A Makefile with 200 targets and no comments.
Valgrind output scrolls endlessly on a cracked monitor.
Header files are stacked floor to ceiling.
The smell of burnt silicon lingers in the air.
rest: A clean compilation with zero warnings. Breathe easy.
A properly null-terminated string to rest against.
An RAII-managed safe zone. Resources will be freed on exit.
treasure: A memory pool with no leaks. Pristine.
A const-correct codebase hides something valuable.
Properly aligned struct padding reveals hidden loot.

floor_flavor: The air is cold and close to the metal...
Compiler warnings echo like distant thunder.
Pointer arithmetic gone wrong has warped the geometry.
You can hear the ticking of an unchecked malloc.
boss_clues: Error messages multiply on the walls...
The stack grows dangerously close to the heap.
Core dump fragments crunch under your feet.
